title: Ctenitis borbonica
taxonomy: Ctenitis borbonica is classified in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family. This family is a part of the larger group known as Polypodiopsida, which is a class of vascular plants that belong to the division Pteridophyta. Within the class Polypodiopsida, the family Dryopteridaceae is a member of the subclass Osmundineae. The species Ctenitis borbonica is further classified as a member of the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical regions. The classification of Ctenitis borbonica reflects its unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.

intro: Ctenitis borbonica, also known as the Bourbon ctenitis or the Pacific polypody, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis borbonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenitis borbonica is commonly used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies in the form of extracts and tinctures, although its use is primarily limited to traditional and local practices.
